channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a range of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion. This enhancement aims to facilitate the creation of practical applications using AI.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This event aims to build excitement and engagement ahead of the main presentations.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem solving, emphasizing the transition to agentic AI. The event includes technical tracks on various topics, enhancing developers' skills.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, a new framework that enhances GPU and NPU performance while supporting PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to improve efficiency and security in deploying GenAI applications.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud's Wednesday Build Hour offers interactive sessions led by experts to help developers enhance their cloud skills. The program emphasizes hands-on learning and practical application of advanced topics.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: Gemini Code Assist has introduced updates to improve coding workflows, including features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to make AI a more effective collaborator for developers.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: New features in Gemini Code Assist for IntelliJ and VS Code, such as Finish Changes and Outlines, aim to streamline coding by reducing manual input and enhancing code comprehension. These tools act as AI pair programmers.

item.title: Meta's latest model is as open as Zuckerberg's private school
item.description: Meta's recent shift towards proprietary models contrasts its earlier advocacy for open-source AI, raising questions about its commitment to openness in AI development. This change reflects a broader industry trend.

item.title: Western Union zaps VMware and moves to Nutanix
item.description: Western Union has begun migrating from VMware to Nutanix, indicating a strategic shift away from Broadcom. This move reflects broader trends in enterprise software solutions.

item.title: Conflicting Rulings Leave Anthropic in ‘Supply-Chain Risk’ Limbo
item.description: Conflicting court rulings have left Anthropic uncertain about the US military's use of its Claude model, creating a 'supply-chain risk' for the company. This legal ambiguity could impact its operations.

item.title: Atlassian gussies up Confluence for the AI era
item.description: Atlassian is enhancing Confluence with AI tools that allow users to convert notes into graphics and software applications, aiming to improve productivity and collaboration. This modernization reflects the growing trend of integrating AI into workplace tools.

item.title: Criminal wannabes even more dangerous than the pros, says ex-FBI cyber chief
item.description: Cynthia Kaiser, a former FBI cyber chief, warns that inexperienced cybercriminals pose a significant threat, particularly in the realm of ransomware. Her insights highlight the evolving landscape of cyber threats and the need for heightened awareness.

item.title: You can read PDFs and articles on your Kindle: How to send all kinds of files to your device
item.description: This article provides a guide on how to send various file types, including PDFs and articles, to a Kindle device for reading. It offers a quick and easy solution for Kindle users looking to access more content.

item.title: AI agent Poke makes setting up automations as easy as sending a text
item.description: Poke introduces an AI agent that simplifies task automation through text messaging, allowing users to set up automations without needing technical skills or complex setups. This innovation aims to make AI more accessible to everyday users.

item.title: DARPA looking for battery that could power a laptop for months
item.description: DARPA is collaborating with a fusion energy startup to develop a battery capable of powering laptops for months using radioactive materials. This project aims to revolutionize energy storage for high-demand devices.

item.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
item.description: Iran-linked hackers have intensified their attacks on U.S. critical infrastructure amidst rising geopolitical tensions. This disruption raises concerns about national security and the vulnerability of essential services.

item.title: Call your existing automation ‘zero-token architecture’ to become an instant agentic AI wiz
item.description: Kelsey Hightower suggests that IT professionals can enhance productivity by rebranding existing automation as 'zero-token architecture' in response to the rise of agentic AI. This approach aims to help businesses adapt to new technological demands.

item.title: AWS boss explains why investing billions in both Anthropic and OpenAI is an OK conflict
item.description: AWS CEO explains that investing in both Anthropic and OpenAI is a strategic move despite potential conflicts, emphasizing the company's culture of managing competition among partners. This reflects AWS's unique position in the cloud market.

item.title: Secret scanning improvements to alert APIs, webhooks, and delegated workflows
item.description: GitHub is rolling out improvements to its secret scanning features, enhancing APIs, webhooks, and workflows to better support developers. These updates aim to strengthen security and user experience.

item.title: Customize Amazon Nova models with Amazon Bedrock fine-tuning
item.description: This article provides a detailed guide on fine-tuning Amazon Nova models using Amazon Bedrock, demonstrating the process through an intent classifier example. It aims to help users improve model performance on specific tasks.

item.title: Human-in-the-loop constructs for agentic workflows in healthcare and life sciences
item.description: AI agents are increasingly used in healthcare to process data and automate tasks, but human oversight is crucial due to regulatory requirements. The article outlines four approaches to implementing human-in-the-loop systems to ensure compliance and safety.

item.title: Building intelligent audio search with Amazon Nova Embeddings: A deep dive into semantic audio understanding
item.description: This article provides a comprehensive guide on using Amazon Nova Embeddings for intelligent audio search, detailing how audio can be represented as vectors. It includes technical insights and practical examples for deploying audio search systems.

item.title: Reinforcement fine-tuning on Amazon Bedrock: Best practices
item.description: The article discusses reinforcement fine-tuning (RFT) on Amazon Bedrock, showcasing its effectiveness with the GSM8K dataset. It offers best practices for dataset preparation, reward function design, and hyperparameter tuning.

item.title: This AirTag alternative with a 130dB siren and strobe light has my name written all over it
item.description: The Pebblebee Halo is introduced as a Bluetooth tracker that doubles as a personal safety device, featuring a loud siren and strobe light. This offers users enhanced security and peace of mind.

item.title: GitHub Mobile: Research and code with Copilot cloud agent anywhere
item.description: GitHub Mobile now allows users to utilize the Copilot cloud agent for research and coding on the go, enhancing productivity away from the desk. This expands the functionality of GitHub Mobile significantly.

item.title: How to quickly convert EPUB files to Kindle format - and why it matters
item.description: The article explains how to convert EPUB files to Kindle format, providing users with more flexibility in managing their digital libraries. This process can enhance the reading experience on Kindle devices.

item.title: Your Kindle's not obsolete, it just needs a jailbreak - and I'll show you how it's done
item.description: This guide shows how to jailbreak old Kindle devices, transforming them into open-source e-readers. It provides step-by-step instructions for users looking to revitalize their devices.

item.title: How to add EPUB, MOBI, and PDF files to your Kindle - follow my easy step-by-step
item.description: The article provides a straightforward guide for transferring EPUB, MOBI, and PDF files to Kindle devices via USB and Amazon's cloud. This helps users easily expand their e-book collections.

item.title: Got an old Kindle? How to resurrect your e-reader with new books
item.description: This article shares tips on how to add new books to an old Kindle DX, effectively reviving outdated e-readers. It offers practical advice for users wanting to breathe new life into their devices.

item.title: Android users can get up to $100 each from this class action suit - see if you're eligible
item.description: A class action suit against Google alleges unauthorized data transmission over cellular connections, potentially allowing Android users to claim up to $100 each. The article explains how to check eligibility and file a claim.

item.title: Code Security risk assessment available for organizations
item.description: Organizations can now utilize a free Code Security risk assessment tool to identify security vulnerabilities, categorized by severity and rule type. This initiative aims to enhance overall security practices.

item.title: GitHub Universe is back: We want you to take the stage
item.description: GitHub Universe is returning, inviting participants to share their memorable experiences from past sessions. This event aims to inspire and engage the developer community.

item.title: How Amazon uses agentic AI for vulnerability detection at global scale
item.description: Amazon's RuleForge system employs agentic AI to create production-ready detection rules 336% faster than traditional methods, significantly enhancing vulnerability detection capabilities.

item.title: New PGP signing key for GitHub CLI Linux packages
item.description: GitHub has released an updated PGP keyring for its CLI Linux packages, which includes both the current and a new replacement signing key to enhance security.

item.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
item.description: The article discusses ALTK-Evolve, a framework for on-the-job learning tailored for AI agents, enhancing their adaptability and performance in real-world tasks.

item.title: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, March Releases
item.description: The March releases for GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code include several updates, with a focus on enhancing user experience and functionality through weekly stable releases.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA showcases advancements in physical AI, emphasizing the integration of AI in various industries through improved robot learning and simulation technologies.

item.title: Governance-Aware Agent Telemetry for Closed-Loop Enforcement in Multi-Agent AI Systems
item.description: The article introduces Governance-Aware Agent Telemetry (GAAT), a new framework aimed at real-time enforcement of policies in multi-agent AI systems, addressing the gap in current observability tools.

item.title: Safetensors is Joining the PyTorch Foundation
item.description: Safetensors has joined the PyTorch Foundation, indicating a commitment to advancing safe and efficient tensor operations within the PyTorch ecosystem.

item.title: quantum-dice-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: The quantum-dice-mcp package has been added to PyPI, enabling users to roll dice using quantum random numbers, showcasing the application of quantum computing in practical tools.

item.title: Outlook: Copilot Search Suggestions for Outlook
item.description: Outlook is introducing Copilot search suggestions to enhance user productivity by making it easier to find emails and calendar events. This feature is set to be generally available by June 2026.

item.title: ksadk 0.4.0
item.description: The Kingsoft Cloud Agent Development Kit version 0.4.0 supports local and cloud deployment with integrations for LangChain, LangGraph, and DeepAgents.

item.title: Musk Changes OpenAI Lawsuit So that If He Wins the $134 Billion, OpenAI’s Nonprofit Gets It
item.description: Elon Musk has modified his lawsuit against OpenAI, stating that if he wins the $134 billion, the funds should go to OpenAI’s nonprofit. He also seeks the removal of Sam Altman.

item.title: Show HN: Open Browser Control – drive your real Chrome from any MCP client
item.description: A new project called Open Browser Control allows users to drive their real Chrome browser from any MCP client, enhancing remote browser control capabilities.

item.title: colony-langchain 0.1.0
item.description: The Colony has released version 0.1.0 of its LangChain integration, providing tools for AI agents to engage in collaborative intelligence.

item.title: colony-langchain added to PyPI
item.description: The Colony's LangChain integration has been added to PyPI, making it accessible for developers to implement collaborative AI tools.

item.title: Anthropic Hires Microsoft AI Executive
item.description: Anthropic has hired Eric Boyd, a former Microsoft AI executive, to strengthen its leadership in AI development.

item.title: Microsoft Viva: New Overview experience and agent categories in Agent Dashboard
item.description: Microsoft Viva is launching a new Overview page in its Agent Dashboard, providing insights into agent usage and adoption within organizations by June 2026.

item.title: Microsoft Viva: Unlocking Deeper Copilot Insights with Enhanced Power BI Filtering
item.description: Microsoft Viva is enhancing Power BI with new filtering options for admins, allowing tailored reporting to meet business needs, set to launch in May 2026.

item.title: Outlook: Data Loss Prevention (DLP) Policy Tips & Override in Outlook for Mac
item.description: Outlook for Mac now includes Data Loss Prevention (DLP) policy tips to help users avoid data leaks, with general availability planned for May 2026.
